sanskrit:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः।
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम्।।
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः।
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये।।
transliteration:
śucau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ |
nātyucchritaṃ nātinicam cailājina-kuśottaram ||
tatraikāgraṃ manaḥ kṛtvā yata-cittendriya-kriyaḥ |
upaviśyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātmaviśuddhaye ||
literal_meaning: In a clean place, establishing a firm seat for oneself, neither too high nor too low, covered with cloth, deer-skin, and kuśa grass; there, making the mind one-pointed, controlling the activities of the mind and senses, sitting on that seat, one should practice yoga for the purification of the self.
contextual_meaning: Creating a psychologically safe, low-stimulus environment is a prerequisite for effective self-regulation and inner work.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative preparation; setting the stage for sustained attention and affect regulation.
trigger_scenario: Turning off phone notifications, clearing clutter, and lighting a candle before starting a 10-minute breathing exercise.
anchor_text: Set a calm seat, steady your mind, and begin inner work.
